data.title: McDonald's review bombed on Google after UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect's arrest
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) restaurants in Altoona, Pennsylvania were review bombed online after a suspect was arrested in connection with the shooting of a UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) executive, prompting Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) to remove the negative reviews.

UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was fatally shot last Wednesday, triggering a nationwide manhunt for the shooter. Luigi Mangione was arrested at a McDonald's (MCD) restaurant in Altoona on Monday after an employee tipped off police. Mangione has been charged with one count of second-degree murder and four other charges.

Thompson's murder unleashed social media rage against U.S. health insurers, with many users showing no sympathy for the executive's death and instead slamming companies for denying claims or care.

Following Mangione's arrest, negative and one-star ratings were posted for around three McDonald's (MCD) locations in or around Altoona, complaining about "snitches."

"This location has rats in the kitchen that will make you sick and your insurance isn't going to cover it," one user posted.

"These reviews violate our policies and have been removed," a Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) spokesperson told Seeking Alpha. According to its policies, reviews must be based on real experiences with a location.

The hostel where Mangione stayed in New York also saw similar negative reviews online, after the NYPD circulated the suspect's photos taken at the hostel's front desk.

Related stories

  • UnitedHealthcare CEO's Death Raises Tough Questions
  • Man questioned in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO
  • UnitedHealth shooting weighs on health insurance stocks
  • UnitedHealthcare CEO killed in 'premeditated' shooting

data.title: Datadog prices $870M convertible senior notes
data.description:
Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) priced $870M aggregate principal amount of 0% convertible senior notes due 2029 in a private placement to qualified institutional buyers.

The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $775M aggregate principal amount of notes.

Initial purchasers have an option to purchase, within a 13-day period up to an additional $130M notes.

The sale of the notes is expected to close on December 12, 2024.

The notes will be general unsecured obligations of Datadog and will not bear regular interest and the principal amount of the notes will not accrete.

Net proceeds will be approximately $851.6M (or ~$979M if the initial purchasers exercise their option to purchase additional notes in full).

Net proceeds from the offering to pay the ~$87.8M cost of the capped call transactions and to repurchase for cash $112M 0.125% convertible senior notes due 2025.

Net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, which may include acquisitions or strategic investments in complementary businesses or technologies, although Datadog does not currently have any plans for any such acquisitions or investments, additional repurchases or repayment of the 2025 notes and working capital, operating expenses and capital expenditures.

data.title: ASE Technology November revenue drops 6.7% sequentially to $1.65B
data.description:
• ASE Technology Holding (NYSE:ASX) reported on Tuesday that November revenue was down 2.6% year-over-year to $1.65 billion, down 6.7% sequentially.
  • Net revenues for ATM assembly, testing, and material business were $909 million, up 6.6% year over year and down 1.0% from last month.

data.title: Riot Platforms prices upsized $525M senior convertible notes
data.description:
• Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) priced its offering of $525M aggregate principal amount of its 0.75% convertible senior notes due 2030.
  • The notes will be sold in a private offering only to qualified institutional buyers.
  • Initial purchasers have an option to purchase, within a three-day period beginning on up to an additional $75M aggregate principal amount of the notes.
  • The offering was upsized from the previously announced offering of $500M notes.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 11, 2024.
  • The notes will be unsecured, senior obligations of Riot. The notes will bear interest at a rate of 0.75% per year, payable semiannually in arrears.

  • Net proceeds will be approximately $511.5M (or ~$584.6M if the initial purchasers exercise in full their option to purchase additional notes).

  • Riot (RIOT) intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to acquire additional bitcoin and for general corporate purposes.

  • The stock price gained ~2.9% on Tuesday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Electric Royalties plans to raise C$4M in private placement
data.description:
• Electric Royalties (TSXV:ELEC:CA) entered into an agreement with Canaccord Genuity and Red Cloud Securities to act as agents, on a "best efforts" basis, in connection with a private placement of up to ~22.22M units at a price of C$0.18 per unit.
  • Gross proceeds expected to be ~C$4M.

  • Each unit will be comprised of one common share and one common share purchase warrant.

  • The net proceeds will be used to complete the acquisition of the 0.75% gross revenue royalty relating to the Punitaqui copper mine in Chile and for general corporate purposes.

  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 20, 2024.

data.title: Protara Therapeutics drops 6%, prices $100M offering
data.description:
• Protara Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TARA) priced its underwritten public offering of 13.69M shares at a price to the public of $6.25 per share and pre-funded warrants to purchase ~2.33M shares of common stock at a price of $6.249 per pre-funded warrant.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional ~2.4M shares.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $100M.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 11, 2024.
  • Net proceeds received from the offering will be to fund the clinical development of TARA-002, as well as the development of other clinical programs. Protara (TARA) may also use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price declined 6% on Monday during after mrket hours of trading.
